real_interview.content: IntroV4Confirmation (hi i'm ellie thanks for coming in today i was created to talk to people in a safe and secure environment i'm not a therapist but i'm here to learn about people and would love to learn about you i'll ask a few questions to get us started and please feel free to tell me anything your answers are totally confidential are you ok with this)
how_doingV (so how are you doing today)
when_LA (when did you move to l_a)
um when i was two and a half years old but actually it wasn't l_a it was santa monica
very_young (you were very young weren't you)
often_backB (how often do you go back to your hometown)
i was only two and a half years old i don't remember anything
like_about_LA (what are some things you really like about l_a)
um the music scene just the general excitement oh and the parks and all that
dont_like_LA (what are some things you don't really like about l_a)
the maintenance on the streets all the pot holes
study (what did you study at school)
well i studied a number of things at school um
um at one point i was i i had majored in public relations journalism specialization in journalism
and i also studied um i was a pre law major
i studied the law and then i also studied um
change_directions (what made you decide to do that)
um mainly because i'm a people person
i don't really have one right now
travel_shoes (i'm sure you can tell by my shoes i'm not much of a world explorer do you travel a lot)
no i don't and i can't even see your shoes
relax_fishtank (sometimes when i'm feeling tense i turn on the fish tank screensaver hey i know it's not hawaii but it's the best i've got what do you do to relax)
watch horror movies i love <luh> i love horror flicks
and i have a fish tank too so i like to watch my fish
also like to play tennis when i can i haven't been able to play lately though because of physical problems
tell_about_that (can you tell me about that)
i had injured uh my my my ankle because i have anemia <aneenia> and um
and i got dizzy one night and i fell on my ankle and on my leg and so i'm just waiting for that to really heal
but i'm planning to get back um into tennis it's one of the things i enjoy doing it really relaxes me
control_temper (how are you at controlling your temper)
i really don't have a temper over the years i've learned how to do how to control that
last_argument (when was the last time you argued with someone and what was it about)
probably would've been yesterday but there was no argument
feelguilty (what's something you feel guilty about)
there's nothing right now that i feel guilty about
situation_handled (tell me about a situation that you wish you had handled differently)
oh a situation that i wish i had handled differently
i really can't think of one right now
hard_decisionB (tell me about the hardest decision you've ever had to make)
the absolute hardest decision that i ever had to make
was when my uh i had twins twin twin twin boys they're grown now
and um i had lost my job and we were living in a motel
the rent for the motel was outrageous i couldn't no longer continue to pay the
um pay the type the type of money that they wanted so i had to briefly um
put my son under the in in foster care for for for a minute
that was the hardest decision that i had to make

um he was born with auditory processing deficit which he overcame through training with a speech therapist and and school officials and he also this is he also lost his hearing when he was four years old
and then when he turned fourteen the schizophrenia came up
um so right now he he's in a supportive living environment he's twenty seven years old
um but he he he's come to terms with his mental illness and he accepts it now
i had problems in the past 'cause he didn't wanna take the medication you'd give him the medication and he'd hide it stick it in his pocket or whatever
so but he's come to terms with that and we've all have come to terms with that and he's doing he's doing pretty good
um that son he's twenty <twen> he's twenty seven as i said 'cause he's a twin
um was living in texas with his wife
and um he got a promotion to um
now he's in california too both are in california okay so
i feel very good about that yeah
easy_parent (do you find it easy to be a parent)
no no no no no no no
parent_hardest (what's the hardest thing about being a parent)
not it's not hard to be a parent of grown of my grown children okay when they were younger there were difficult times okay difficult periods and um
just it was a lot of good times too but yeah it was there were some difficult times
tell_me_moreV2 (can you tell me more about that)
well the mm the difficult times i really pretty much already alluded to and that was when um one of my twins got the diagnosis of schizophrenic and um
in in well one was in foster care and one was actually um in a institution 'cause of the um the um schizophrenia
think i lost track of the question <laughter>
parent_hardest (what's the hardest thing about being a parent)
the hardest thing about being a parent
at this point at this <the> at this point in my life there is is nothing hard about it
parent_best (what's the best thing about being a parent)
the best thing about being a parent
i don't know i guess it's just to see <cough>
something that you created um develop into a you know pretty decent human being you know a decent person
parent_differences (what are some ways that you're different as a parent than your parents)
i didn't have two parents i had a mother
and the father was never in my father was never in the home and he lived in another state so i had no contact with him um
but the thing that i do
differently than what my mother did
she at she had this tendency which she outgrew
she had this tendency to be very critical okay critical and um
sort of looked at people who were not and that was me um as if they
were living life wrong because they were not going to church or reading the bible and that kind of thing
easy_sleep (how easy is it for you to get a good night's sleep)
i i i don't get a good night's sleep or i don't sleep at all unless i take medication and that's that that's all there is to it if i don't take medication i don't sleep i'll be up all night i'll
i will be up for days if i don't take medication
sleep_affects (what are you like when you don't sleep well)
feel_lately (how have you been feeling lately)
you know just a little anxious really um
you know um i just recently um
made a move to a new apartment it's a lot of expenses and
new expenses and things like that you know and new furniture and new this and that that you have to get which um i've i managed to do all of that but
um the whole thing was kind of stressful
trade_offs (there are always trade-offs in life aren't there)
ptsd_diagnosed (have you ever been diagnosed with p_t_s_d)
depression_diagnosed (have you been diagnosed with depression)
when_diagnosed (how long ago were you diagnosed)
um that was back in i think two thousand and four
why_seek_help (what got you to seek help)
because um my life had gotten so stressful
it was either i had to do something or
or end something to tell you the truth yeah
therapy_going (do you still go to therapy now)
therapy_useful (do you feel like therapy is useful)
yeah i think it's useful um you know as a good sounding board
you know your concerns out and you know discuss things that may be bothering you or good things too yeah i think therapy with medication
therapy_changes (what sort of changes have you noticed since you've been going to therapy)
since i've been going to therapy
it it's just been a good experience for me which is why i continue to go
happy_lasttime (tell me about the last time you felt really happy)
um by some occurrence i can't think of anything right now just like that
i go back to how i was feeling
so i can't really pinpoint anything really i mean
eh eh i mean if something comes to me unexpectedly or if things go right for a change and continue to go right as opposed to things going wrong all the time
that that makes me happy when things go right how about that
Ellie17Dec2012_10 (tell me about something you did recently that you really enjoyed)
something i did recently that i really enjoyed
BF_describe (how would your best friend describe you)
i don't have a best friend
Ellie17Dec2012_07 (what would you say are some of your best qualities)
i genuinely give people the benefit of the doubt
is as they appear to be
until they prove prove it differently
and i'm very i like to help other people
i mean help you know i'm on a personal level help people
you know with anything whatever their problem is it gives me pleasure to do something for somebody else that's helping them out
there's only one thing i regret and that is that
after my twins became um became old enough to um
start going to um school the elementary school level um
i was taking some courses um as a pre law major
at that time i was married to their father
who who turned out not to be a good
because of the stresses involved with him
you know knowing that that was gonna end and i was gonna leave him
so that was my biggest regret
advice_back (what advice would you give to yourself ten or twenty years ago)
stay in school no matter what
stay the course no matter what
Ellie17Dec2012_08 (what are you most proud of in your life)
asked_everything (okay i think i have asked everything i need to)
appreciate_open (thanks for sharing your thoughts with me)
